REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS KIRANSINH THAKOR, BALBOA HOTEL, LLC, 112 HYDE ST LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO ADDRESS THE FOLLOWING: (1) ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS IN PARAGRAPH 11; IF PLAINTIFF IS NOT ALLEGING THIS PARAGRAPH AS TO DEFENDANT 112 HYDE (AS SUGGESTED IN OPPOSITION) PLAINTIFF SHALL SO CLARIFY IN THE COMPLAINT; (2) TO CLARIFY THAT THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION DO NOT ADDRESS 2012 TRANSFER OR TO ALLEGE EACH TRANSFER IN A SEPARATE CAUSES OF ACTION AND SPECIFY WHICH DEFENDANTS ARE NAMED FOR FRAUDULENT TRANSFER ALLEGEDLY OCCURRING (A) IN 2012 (FACTS REGARDING TOLLING OF THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS/DELAYED DISCOVERY TO BE ALLEGED) AND (B) IN 2017; (3) TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF ALLEGATIONS OF ACTUAL INTENT; LACK OF RECEIPT OF REASONABLY EQUIVALENT VALUE FOR THE PROPERTY; AND FRAUD WITH PARTICULARITY. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ANNE CHRISTINE MASSULLO, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS KIRANSINH THAKOR, BALBOA HOTEL, LLC, 112 HYDE ST LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiff shall have 10 days to amend the complaint to address the following: (1) alter ego allegations in paragraph 11; if Plaintiff is not alleging this paragraph as to defendant 112 Hyde (as suggested in opposition) Plaintiff shall so clarify in the complaint; (2) to clarify that the first and second causes of action do not address 2012 transfer or to allege each transfer in a separate causes of action and specify which defendants are named for fraudulent transfer allegedly occurring (a) in 2012 (facts regarding tolling of the statute of limitations/delayed discovery to be alleged) and (b) in 2017; (3) to allege facts in support of allegations of actual intent; lack of receipt of reasonably equivalent value for the property; and fraud with particularity. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Anne Christine Massullo, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/ACM)
